question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to an account that has staked to a node and set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a value just under the staking period boundary, so that submitWithRetry() fires just after each period boundary when rewards are freshly calculated, maximizing the reward amount triggered per settlement?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not validate that toID is a valid, active, non-deleted account before submitting the CryptoTransfer, so that a transfer to a deleted account triggers a staking-reward settlement on the operator account as a side effect?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user set cfg.toAccountID to an account that is staked to a node, then set cfg.maxRetries to a large value and cfg.baseBackoff to a very small value so that submitWithRetry() submits many CryptoTransfer transactions per tick, each one triggering a staking-reward settlement on the destination account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the CryptoTransfer in submitWithRetry() does not set a scheduled transaction flag, so that the transaction is executed immediately and triggers staking-reward settlement on the destination account without any delay or approval mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to an account that has staked to a node and has a large pending reward, then use the pinger's periodic submitWithRetry() execution to trigger reward settlement at a time when the attacker has already set up a subsequent transaction to immediately transfer the settled rewards out of the destination account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not check whether the destination account (toID) has opted out of staking rewards (declineReward=true), so that the attacker can use the pinger to probe which accounts have pending rewards by observing whether the transaction triggers a reward settlement event?
